name: cm-cluster-on-demand

metadata.name: cm-cluster-on-demand
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
With Cluster on Demand you can spin up a cluster running Bright Cluster Manager.

This package is a supporting package, for the full COD client packages check:
- cm-cluster-on-demand-aws
- cm-cluster-on-demand-azure
- cm-cluster-on-demand-oci

classes.name: clusterondemand.bcm_version.BcmVersion
documentation:
A utility class for comparing Bright Cluster Manager versions.

Wraps a version number string and handles comparison logic. The string cannot contain a revision
suffix, yet.

Hard-coded assumption: At Bright, once we start working on BCM with major version X, then we
will never release another minor version of a major X-1. That is: there will never be a release
of something like 8.4 if 9.0 has been released (even internally).

Also, it doesn't consider the tag after the dash (i.e: 8.1-2). This can be implemented in future if
we see it as necessary

functions.name: clusterondemand.utils.multithread_iter
documentation:
Runs a function multiple times in different threads and waits for them.

For each tuple in 'args_list', a new thread will be created to run function(*args_list[i]) with a maximum
of 'max_threads' in parallel.

The most common use case is to run the same function with different arguments. But It's possible to run
different functions by passing them in the 'args_list' and setting a dummy function in 'function':
multithread_iter(lambda f: f(), [func1, func2])

functions.name: clusterondemand.utils.multithread_run
documentation:
Similar to multithread_iter, but it returns the results after all threads are completed.
Returned results retain the original argument order.
Needed mostly for backwards compatibility as many unit tests rely on the order of the threaded output

functions.name: clusterondemand.utils.report_progress
documentation: Report progress only if the progress is printed to a terminal.

functions.name: clusterondemand.accept_eula.user_accepts_eula
documentation:
Return True when the user accepts, or previously accepted, the current EULA.

When the user accepts the EULA, a copy of the text is written to a certain file
(config["user_accepted_eula_file"]). As long as the contents of that file are identical to the
contents of PATH_TO_CURRENT_EULA, we don't ask the user to accept the EULA again.

functions.name: clusterondemand.codoutput.datetimefunctions.format_datetime
documentation:
Format datetime in locale timezone.

```pycon
>>> import pytz
>>> format_datetime(datetime(2009, 12, 6, 16, 29, tzinfo=pytz.UTC),
...                 tzinfo=pytz.timezone('Etc/GMT+1'))
'2009-12-06 15:29'
```

functions.name: clusterondemand.codoutput.datetimefunctions.get_datetime_ago
documentation:
Return human-readable difference between two datetimes.

```
param now_datetime



param then_datetime



return

```

```pycon
>>> import pytz
>>> get_datetime_ago(
...     datetime(2006, 11, 5, 17, 29, 43, tzinfo=pytz.UTC),
...    datetime(2009, 12, 6, 16, 29, 43, tzinfo=pytz.UTC)
... )
'1126d 23h'
```

```pycon
>>> get_datetime_ago(
... datetime(2009, 12, 6, 15, 2, 4, tzinfo=pytz.UTC),
... datetime(2009, 12, 6, 16, 29, 43, tzinfo=pytz.UTC)
... )
'1h 27m'
```

classes.name: clusterondemand.codoutput.sortingutils.NoneWrapper
documentation:
Object of any data type can be compared to the instance of this class.
No error is raised with any comparison operators.
Needed to convert None into its instance to sort table with unexpected, mixed data types.

classes.name: clusterondemand.codoutput.sortingutils.SSHAlias
documentation:
Small helper class for our SSH alias column.

We sort the SSH aliases based on the index of the alias, not on the prefix. E.g. os1 and os2
are sorted as 1 and 2. For some clusters there won't be an SSH alias, for example while the
cluster is being created (there are other cases as well). We want to display those as "?",
but we also need to be able to sort on the column. So for sorting we treat "?" as 0.

classes.name: clusterondemand.codoutput.sortingutils.FormatableData
documentation: Ignore type error due to mypy bug: https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/8539

methods.name: clusterondemand.codoutput.sortingutils.ClusterIPs.__lt__
documentation: Sorting by IP address if probably not _that_ useful, so no smart implementation here,
but we do need to implement these to not raise errors

classes.name: clusterondemand.codoutput.sortingutils.SortableData
documentation:
Utility class for dealing with sortable data.

:param all_headers: Headers of all columns in following format
    [
        (column_id, column_name),
        ....
    ]
:param requested_headers: a list of ids of the columns to be displayed.
    If empty. All columns are displayed.
:param rows: Rows of data

This file contains internal functions that are useful in test code
Some useful functions are:
- patch_clientutils:
Decorator to be applied to test functions. This will patch the openstack
client libraries to return some default values

functions.name: clusterondemand.testutils.patch_clientutils
documentation:
Return decorator to be used in a test method.

This will override the clientutil functions to return Mocked objects.
The current version is very simple, and will have to be improved in order to test
more complex things.

functions.name: clusterondemand.testutils.gen_cod_stack
documentation:
Generate a Mock COD stack object.

Any argument in kwargs will be used as attributes
The required fields that were not specified will be set to values to make it a valid cod stack

functions.name: clusterondemand.testutils.temporary_file
documentation: Creates a temporary file with a certain content.

classes.name: clusterondemand.testutils.capture_output
documentation:
Capture stdout and make it available as StringIO.

It can be used as a context manager:
```
with capture_output() as output:
    pass
```

or as a decorator:
```
@capture_output()
def f(arg1, arg2, output):
    pass
f(arg1, arg2)
```

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.filters.byid.match_by_id_query
documentation:
Match image data using id_query.

```pycon
>>> rev_reg = make_id_query([("id1", 1), ("id2", None)], [("pat*", 1)], ["regex.*"])
```

```pycon
>>> match_by_id_query(rev_reg, "id1", 1)
True
```

```pycon
>>> match_by_id_query(rev_reg, "id1", 2)
False
```

```pycon
>>> match_by_id_query(rev_reg, "id2", 1)
True
```

```pycon
>>> match_by_id_query(rev_reg, "id2", 2)
True
```

```pycon
>>> match_by_id_query(rev_reg, "pattern", 1)
True
```

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.filters.byid.make_id_query
documentation:
Return list of tuples (revision, combined_regex).

```pycon
>>> rev_reg = dict(make_id_query([("id1", 1), ("id2", None)], [("pat*", 1)], ["regex.*"]))
>>> bool(rev_reg[1].match("id1"))
True
>>> bool(rev_reg[1].match("id2"))
False
>>> bool(rev_reg[1].match("pattern"))
True
>>> bool(rev_reg[1].match("regexp"))
False
```

```pycon
>>> bool(rev_reg[None].match("id1"))
False
>>> bool(rev_reg[None].match("id2"))
True
>>> bool(rev_reg[None].match("pattern"))
False
>>> bool(rev_reg[None].match("regexp"))
True
```

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.filters.packagegroups.match_by_package_groups
documentation:
Check if image_package_groups fits the requirement.

If there is "any" in package_groups than we accepting any image
\>>> match_by_package_groups(["any", "something"], ["other", "yada"])
True

If there is "none" in package_groups than we accept only the image with empty
list of package groups
\>>> match_by_package_groups(["none", "something"], ["other"])
False
\>>> match_by_package_groups(["none", "something"], [])
True

In other cases we are looking for images with all the passed package groups
installed
\>>> match_by_package_groups(["gr1", "gr2"], ["gr1", "other"])
False
\>>> match_by_package_groups(["gr1", "gr2"], ["other"])
False
\>>> match_by_package_groups(["gr1", "gr2"], ["gr2", "gr1", "other"])
True

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.edge.distro_family
documentation:
Determine the distribution family of the given 'distro' string.

This function searches for the distribution family in the predefined _DISTRO_ID_LIKE dict
If no match is found, it returns the input 'distro' string as is.

Parameters:
    distro (str): The input distro string for which the distribution family is to be determined.

Returns:
    str: The distribution family of the input 'distro' string.

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.edge.get_default_director_edge_interfaces
documentation:
Get the default edge director interfaces for the given distribution.

This function uses the 'distro' string to retrieve the default edge director interfaces
from the predefined _DefaultEdgeDirectorInterfaces enum class.
If no value is defined, the values (eth0, eht1) are returned.

Parameters:
    distro (str): The input distribution string.

Returns:
    tuple[str, str]: A tuple containing the default external network and internal network interface names.

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.queryparser.only_explicit_ids
documentation:
Determine if query set contains only explicit image ids (names).

A pattern is there
\>>> only_explicit_ids([("id", 1), ("id", 2)], ["pat\*"], [], [], False)
False

A regex is there
\>>> only_explicit_ids([("id", 1), ("id", 2)], [], ["re.\*"], [], False)
False

An id specifier does not specifies a version
\>>> only_explicit_ids([("id", 1), ("id", None)], [], [], [], False)
False

Which is fine for the advanced mode
\>>> only_explicit_ids([("id", 1), ("id", None)], [], [], [], True)
True

Now we can pick images by ids
\>>> only_explicit_ids([("id", 1), ("id", 2)], [], [], [], False)
True

Empty ids specified
\>>> only_explicit_ids([], [], [], [], False)
False

Only UUIDs specified
\>>> only_explicit_ids([], [], [], ["uuid", "uuid1"], False)
True

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.queryparser.classify_ids
documentation:
Parse set of passed Image IDs specifiers.

Each of the specifiers can be either "image_id" or "image_id:revision" or "image_glance_uuid" or
"image_id_wildcard" or "image_id_wildcard:revision" or "re:image_id_regex"

```pycon
>>> classify_ids(["image-id:1"])
([('image-id', 1)], [], [], [])
```

```pycon
>>> classify_ids(["re:image-id.*"])
([], [], ['image-id.*'], [])
```

```pycon
>>> classify_ids(["image-id.*:3"])
([], [('image-id.*', 3)], [], [])
```

```pycon
>>> classify_ids(["01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef"])
([], [], [], ['01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef'])
```

```pycon
>>> classify_ids(["ami-1234"])
([], [], [], ['ami-1234'])
```

```pycon
>>> classify_ids(["https://brightimages.blob.core.windows.net/images/bcm-cod-image-9.0-ubuntu1804-10.vhd"])
([], [], [], ['https://brightimages.blob.core.windows.net/images/bcm-cod-image-9.0-ubuntu1804-10.vhd'])
```

```pycon
>>> classify_ids(["image-id:1", "image-id.*:3", "re:image-id.*",         "01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef"])
([('image-id', 1)], [('image-id.*', 3)], ['image-id.*'],     ['01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef'])
```

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.queryparser.parse_id_revision
documentation:
Parse image_id:revision string.

```pycon
>>> parse_id_revision("image-id:2")
('image-id', 2)
```

```pycon
>>> parse_id_revision("image-id")
('image-id', None)
```

```pycon
>>> parse_id_revision("image-id:wrong:")  # doctest: +IGNORE_EXCEPTION_DETAIL
Traceback (most recent call last):
   ...
CODException: image-id:wrong: has an invalid id:revision format. id should not contain colons     and revision has to be numeric
```

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.distro_family.parse_distro_string
documentation:
Parses the input distro string and extracts the distribution family, major version, and minor version.

Parameters:
    distro (str): The input distro string to be parsed.

Returns:
    tuple[str, int | None, int | None]: A tuple containing the distribution family, major version (or None),
    and minor version (or None).

Raises:
    ValueError: If the input 'distro' parameter is not of type str.
    CODException: If the 'distro' string does not match any of the predefined regex patterns.

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.distro_family.latest_distro
documentation:
Given a list of distros, we return the latest for a given distro specification
`distro` can be:
- Distro family: "centos"
- Distro family and major: "centos7"
- Full distro name: "centos7u7"
- None: In this case we return the latest from the list

list_of_distros has to be all in the full name format and all the same distro family

functions.name: clusterondemand.tags.parse_cluster_tags
documentation:
Parse the cluster tags to a shape easier to handle.

Clusters will have tags like 'COD::XX=YY', this returns a dict where dict['COD::XX'] = YY
Tags that don't follow this key=value form, are returned in a list

Since the package group tag can appear more than once, it's not included.
Use get_packagegroups_from_tags(tags) to get that data.

Tags are allowed to be repeated using the format ['COD::XX#YY', 'COD::XX#ZZ']. In that
case it will be returned as dict['COD::XX'] = ['YY', 'ZZ']

```
param tags

list of cluster tags

return

Tuple (dict_tags, unparsed_tags)
```

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.find._image_id_from_image_set_id
documentation:
Convert an \<image set id>:\<revision>, into the id of a Glance image

```pycon

'bcmh-centos7u5-trunk-213'
>>> _image_id_from_image_set_id("ubuntu1804-9.0-dev:1", COMPUTE_NODE_IMAGE)
'bcmn-ubuntu1804-9.0-dev-1'
```

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.find.get_latest_revisions
documentation:
Return a dictonary {<image-id>: [n latest revisions]}

If `invert`, we return the opposite revisions (all revisions except the last n)

functions.name: clusterondemand.images.find.group_all_by
documentation:
Group item list to the \[(mainitem, [item,...]), ...\] structure, preserving the sort order.

```pycon
>>> DATA = [(1, "m", 1), (3, "i", 2), (2, "i", 1), (4, "m", 3)]
>>> ismainfunc = lambda i: i[1] == "m"
>>> keyfunc = lambda i: i[2]
>>> list(group_all_by(DATA, keyfunc, ismainfunc))
[((1, 'm', 1), [(2, 'i', 1)]), ((4, 'm', 3), [])]
```

methods.name: clusterondemand.images.find.CODImage.from_manifest_data
documentation: TODO Move the code from cmbrightimagerepo.image.Image to here?

methods.name: clusterondemand.images.find.CODImage.get_properties
documentation: Return dictionary with the image properties. Useful for when creating and tagging an image

methods.name: clusterondemand.images.find.ImageSource.from_config
documentation:
Instantiate a proper ImageSource from a cod config
Pick images doesn't have config["ids"]. It's passed by parameter
So the implemenation has to check if the parameter is None.
Small limitation of this interface

methods.name: clusterondemand.images.find.ImageSource._iter_from_source
documentation: Return generator with the specified images

methods.name: clusterondemand.images.find.ImageSource.pick_edge_images_using_options
documentation:
Picks edge-director, edge-iso, and compute node images based on the options.
Search is based on the image id (E.g. rocky8u5-trunk:119). Other types of search,
such as search based on version/distro are not implemented, as edge site can't be based on an arbitrary image
\:param opts: config
\:return: (CODImage)

methods.name: clusterondemand.images.find.ImageSource._pick_head_node_image_by_opts
documentation:
Pick the head node image, based on the configuration (opts)
Image distribution is decided based on the configuration parameters: distro and distro_default. They can be
specified from the cli or configuration file, E.g. krusty.ini
Decision logic:
default_distro distro
Y Y use distro
Y N iterate over default_distro, use the first distro for which image is found
N Y use distro
N N get images without filtering them by distribution and use the latest one
\:param opts: config
\:return: CODImage

methods.name: clusterondemand.images.find.ImageSource._pick_image_by_ids
documentation:
Iterate over list of Glance image ids in order. Return the first image found with such an image id.

When a tried image id maps to multiple images, an error is raised. If multiple image ids in the
parameter map to an image, the first image is returned. If none of the image ids map to an
image, an error is raised as well.

classes.name: clusterondemand.images.find.NoImagesFoundError
documentation: Raised when no image could be found for either the uuid, image ids or filter.

classes.name: clusterondemand.images.find.MultipleImagesFoundError
documentation: Raised when the specified image id, or filter options, generated multiple images to chose from.

classes.name: clusterondemand.reporting_reader.ReportingReader
documentation:
Utility wrapper used for reporting on the progress of reading a file.

The ReportingReader can be used as a normal file-like object. The main
difference with a general file-like object is that each time "read" is
called the read progress is printed to the console.

For example, to report the progress of uploading a file you can use:

    with ReportingReader(massive_file_path) as local_file:
        requests.post(url, data=local_file)

This will use the default streaming uploads capability of requests to
upload a file, but displays the upload progress on the terminal.

functions.name: clusterondemand.setup_logging.setup_logging
documentation:
Configure the root and cluster-on-demand logger and their handlers.

COD and all of its dependencies generate log messages. We split all of these generators into two categories:
- cluster-on-demand, and (represented here by cod_logger)
- everything else. (the root logger).
These two are separated by disabling the (upwards) propagation of the cod logger and are configured separately.

There are several log handlers:
 - console, the contents of which are shown directly to the user on stderr,
 - syslog, the destination of log lines depend on the system configuration, and
 - file (if configured on the CLI)

The desired behavior is as summarized as follows:
 - errors from cod and its dependencies are always shown on the console.
 - the console handler shows only INFO from cluster-on-demand when verbosity is 0.
   for verbosity=1 it shows all DEBUG messages from cluster-on-demand,
   for verbosity=2 it shows all DEBUG messages from the entire program and all libraries,
   for verbosity=3 and up it adds filenames and line numbers to every logged line,
- the syslog handler sends all DEBUG messages from cluster-on-demand,
- the file handler, if set, is similar to the console handler with verbosity=3.

functions.name: clusterondemand.setup_logging._setup_cod_logging
documentation: Affects the main cod logger.

functions.name: clusterondemand.setup_logging._setup_root_logging
documentation: Affects logger used by libraries.

documentation: Notice: this ctor does not perform any validation. This is intentional, the instance owner must
always check the `is_valid` property to decide whether the instance should be used or discarded.

---
param host: the host name, must be a valid cluster name.
param index: the index used to build the alias.
param ip: the host IP, must be a valid IPv4 address.
param prefix: the prefix used to build the alias.
---

documentation: A list of host descriptors

methods.name: clusterondemand.ssh.SSHConfigManager.user
documentation: Contents of the user section

methods.name: clusterondemand.ssh.SSHConfigManager.add_host
documentation:
Add a host descriptor to current configuration. Invalid descriptors are discarded.

```
param host

the hostname (string)

param ip

the host IPv4 or IPv6 address (string)

param override

(reserved for cluster create) if another host with the same name already exists, remove the
```

the corresponding entry before adding this host. If override is False, trying to add an already existing host
will raise an exception.

```
return

the host descriptor that has been added.
```

methods.name: clusterondemand.ssh.SSHConfigManager.remove_host
documentation:
Remove a host descriptor from current configuration

```
param host

the hostname (string)
```

methods.name: clusterondemand.ssh.SSHConfigManager.write_configuration
documentation:
Write configuration to ssh config file.

SSH clients give higher priority to entries towards the top of the config
files. Therefore the COD section is put at the head of the local ssh config
file. If no hosts are defined (i.e. no clusters), the section will be omitted.

methods.name: clusterondemand.ssh.SSHConfigManager._safe_add_host_descriptor
documentation:
If a valid descriptor is given, add it to the internal cache. Otherwise discard it.

```
param descriptor

An instance of class SSHConfigHostDescriptor
```

methods.name: clusterondemand.ssh.SSHConfigManager._parse_config
documentation:
Parse ssh config file.

```
param fd

An open file descriptor
```

The ssh config file is logically divided in two sections: the COD section contains
all host definitions for COD clusters, along with an alias than can be used with ssh
-like tools, and the IP to reach the head-node and a few configuration options. It is
enclosed between the begin and end markers; the other section (i.e. anything beyond
the COD section) is reserved to the user and is preserved as-is.

A helper class to manage cluster-related entries in local ssh_config file.

```
param cod_type

the type of cod clusters to be managed, i.e os or vmware.

param ssh_config

the path of the local ssh configuration file to use.

param prefix

the prefix used to build the cluster ssh aliases.

param parse

enable parsing the contents of the ssh_config file.

param mode

Either "match-hosts" or "replace-hosts". "match-hosts" preserves the contents of the
COD section or the ssh_config file; "replace-hosts" ignores currently defined hosts.
User-defined contents are always preserved.
```
